  "findings": [
    "Excessive parasitic current draw draining the battery while the vehicle is off",
    "Improper diagnostic sequence can wake up modules and skew readings"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Perform parasitic draw test using the correct sequence (SEQ) to isolate the faulty module or circuit",
    "Measure voltage drop across fuses to identify the active circuit without waking up the CAN bus"
  ],
  "rationale": "The diagnostic procedure for parasitic draw must follow a strict sequence (SEQ) to ensure modules enter sleep mode. Pulling fuses directly can wake up modules on the CAN bus, leading to false readings. The correct sequence involves latching door switches, waiting for sleep timers, and measuring voltage drop across fuses to isolate the degraded or failed component causing the draw.",
  "references": [
    "OEM Service Manual - Electrical Diagnostics",
    "OBD-II Standards - Module Sleep/Wake States"
  ]
}
